title: [HW] InstanceOp verifier failure
body:
If I create a module with parameter as:
```
hw.module @test<param: i32>(in in0: !esi.channel<!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"param">>>, out: ...) { ... }
```
Then instantiate it using a `!esi.channel`, say `channel0`:
```
%outputs = hw.instance "test" @test<param: i32 = 1>(in0: channel0 : !esi.channel<i1>) -> (out: ...)
```
Then this error will show up:
```
'hw.instance' op operand type #0 must be '!esi.channel<!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"param">>>', but got '!esi.channel<i1>'
```
However, if I directly use `!hw.int<#hw.param.decl.ref<"param">>` instead of wrap this in other types like `!esi.channel`, it will work with no problems. I guess there is something missing in the verifier of InstanceOp, specifically in the `evaluateParametricType` function, in which there should be another Case for `esi::ChannelType`?
Any help would be great!
